title: While Sri Lanka’s woes are as much of its own making as fueled by global trends, they are an ominous marker of what’s to come in a world that seems able to handle just one crisis at a time—and often not even that.
body_lines: Many Western leaders are behaving as though there is one crisis in the world: Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. While some are waking up to the widespread knock-on effects for food and energy security, there is little bandwidth, it seems, to address the underlying looming crisis: a global economic unwinding driven by the COVID-19 pandemic, climate breakdown, and degradation of the international political and economic system that has been at least a decade in the making.

Together, these crises have put scores of countries at serious risk and lit a fuse where those risks intersect with authoritarianism and poor governance. Sri Lanka is a case in point. The country owes over $50 billion to government creditors such as India, China, and Japan, and private bondholders—and it is no longer making interest payments.

The reasons for its economic woes are complex. Sri Lankan President Gotabaya Rajapaksa’s family has dominated the country for much of its recent history through a populist, security-centered regime criticized for economic mismanagement, corruption, and its brutal campaign to end the civil war in 2009. Its conduct during the conflict has been the subject of several United Nations reports, which point to credible allegations of war crimes and human rights abuses.

Under the Rajapaksa family’s rule, Sri Lanka has incurred a string of Chinese debts, including for white elephant projects that have yielded little to no income—one such project close to the Rajapaksas’ hometown was dubbed “The World’s Emptiest International Airport” by Forbes. When COVID-19 struck, they ploughed on with sweeping tax cuts as tourism collapsed—wiping out state revenue and personal incomes.

Two years on, the country has run out of foreign exchange. Last week, it was down to its last 24 hours of gasoline stocks. Medicine and food supplies are critically low. Despite ample warning and mounting protests, the government held out for too long before approaching the International Monetary Fund (IMF), and none of the structural and governance reforms are in place that might allow an IMF program a realistic chance of success. Instead, the government continues to struggle under a caretaker setup that lacks a clear public mandate.

While Sri Lanka’s woes are as much of its own making as fueled by global trends, they are an ominous marker of what’s to come in a world that seems able to handle just one crisis at a time—and often not even that.

Rising food prices and shortages, a consequence of the Russia-Ukraine war and COVID-19, as well as security-induced supply chain disruptions, are driving large parts of the developing world toward a cost-of-living and sovereign debt crisis with likely drastic consequences.

According to Bloomberg, “At least 14 developing economies … have debt yields at an excess of 1,000 basis points over US Treasuries, a threshold for bonds to be considered distressed.” The U.N. has warned that 1.7 billion people in 107 countries are at serious risk of food, fuel, and financial insecurity: a fifth of the global population and more than half of the organization’s member states.

Each country has a different story for why it faces turmoil. Ghana, a well-managed economy with generally good governance, is in trouble because of its virtues. It could borrow commercially, so it did, and it now faces a debt crisis. Rich countries are also not immune. In March, high energy costs led to strikes and protests in France, Greece, and Spain.

Egypt, a former wheat exporter, is now the world’s biggest importer as climate pressures combine with dramatic price increases due to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Before the crisis, Russia and Ukraine supplied 30 percent of global wheat exports, and Ukraine is also one of the World Food Program’s biggest suppliers of sunflower oil and other foods.

Many of these countries are likely to end up in the same emergency ward. And, a bit like our health services in the face of COVID-19, our global financial hospital is overwhelmed, with equipment (funds) and staff (leaders) in short supply.

Multilateral bodies such as the U.N. and World Bank must act to prevent widespread suffering while supporting political, economic, and governance reforms that citizens across developing countries are demanding.

In Sri Lanka, as with other countries that will find themselves in similar circumstances, the U.N., the World Bank, and the Asian Development Bank can all take action to mitigate the crisis in the short term. But, in a country like Sri Lanka with a history of diverting aid, they must align the terms of their support to ensure goods and services are delivered in a transparent and accountable manner—and reach all communities. Otherwise, they risk bailing out corrupt politicians instead of people in need.

In the medium term, this relief must include an offramp that allows for a credible government with broad support to enact policies that will create a viable economic and political future for Sri Lanka. Here, the IMF plays a key role. It should see the Sri Lankan people as its partners and ensure that the governance reforms included as part of its package reflect their will. The World Bank, in turn, can help with technical support and financing to help these reforms become reality.

Already, civil society groups have called for sensible measures such as eliminating the much-maligned executive presidency, increasing the independence of institutions, empowering anti-corruption bodies, and going after stolen government assets. Calls for justice for the tens of thousands of people killed, tortured, and disappeared in Sri Lanka are also growing louder. The IMF should take heed and consider the people its true partner in any deal—in any country.  After all, it is the public that will ultimately repay the IMF.

In the longer term, Sri Lanka and other nations will require debt restructuring. Here, creditor nations, and the G-20 in particular, must improve upon the existing “Common Framework,” which has offered too little, too late, and nothing at all to middle-income countries such as Sri Lanka. At a minimum, any country that enters restructuring should expect an immediate debt repayment standstill and ultimately debt reductions matched to a realistic plan for economic growth. Private creditors and China must participate, and if they do not, the IMF should step in to lend to the country anyway, on the condition that those holding out are not repaid.

Such action is predicated on political support by rich countries. But too many governments are looking away, trying to ride a wave of patriotism over Ukraine but without the vision, will, skill, or bandwidth to respond at scale on this other front, which threatens global stability perhaps as much as Russia’s invasion. To win, we need to prevail on both.

Last week, G-20 finance ministers missed an opportunity for progress on debt. While the United States led an important debate at the U.N. on the food crisis, the discussion descended into a blame game. The measures touted are welcome, but they will surely be greeted with cynicism by people still waiting for COVID-19 vaccines, let alone debt relief and climate finance.

In 2021, the IMF issued a record $650 billion of Special Drawing Rights to boost global liquidity by giving IMF members a line of credit they can use to borrow against. But rich-country pledges to make such funds available to low-income countries have fallen far short of this, and not a cent has been disbursed.

Even Afghanistan, which dominated the news cycle last year, appears largely forgotten, with the U.N.’s humanitarian appeal only half funded as 95 percent of people there go hungry, with shocking reports of people selling children and organs. Meanwhile, countries like Chad and South Sudan rarely make it onto the global agenda.

We must be able to focus on more than one thing at the same time. And yet we continue to prevaricate on mechanisms that would increase our capacity for response, such as a global emergency platform that would convene stakeholders from all sectors, or flexible funding to ensure humanitarian aid is not predicated on media coverage. Programs to support human rights and democratic governance are woefully underfunded.

Here in the United Kingdom, the omens are less than good: A government that has been an effective cheerleader on Ukraine has announced a dismal new development strategy that mimics, on a much smaller scale, what it sees in China’s aid and trade model. Foreign Secretary Liz Truss says the U.K. won’t mirror the tactics of “malign actors” but that it will “match them.”

If this reading is uncharitable, so is the U.K.’s decision to desert the anti-poverty work for which it was renowned and nearly halve its multilateral aid spending—while calling on international institutions to “scale up” their response to the global food crisis.

This is compounded by yet another ideologically driven exercise to slash the civil service, having already shuttered its international development ministry and folded the sorry remains into the Foreign Office. Sri Lanka is not the only country that must ensure its quest for stability does not entrench the failing regimes that have led to the current plight.

These challenges are immense: a world of constrained resources, diminished governmental capacity, and mounting domestic issues in the European Union and United States, such as the resurgent threat posed by former U.S. President Donald Trump to the Biden administration. The signs are inauspicious: Efforts to restore international COVID-19 funding were stymied again by the U.S. Congress last week. Washington and its Western counterparts are one-issue towns at present. As crises multiply, they cannot afford to be this short-sighted.

si.title: ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දුක්ඛිත තත්ත්වයන් ගෝලීය ප්‍රවණතා මගින් පෝෂණය වන තරමටම එය විසින්ම සාදන ලද ඒවා වන අතර, ඒවා වරකට එක් අර්බුදයක් පමණක් හසුරුවා ගැනීමට හැකි බව පෙනෙන ලෝකයක ඉදිරියට එන දේ පිළිබඳ අශුභ සලකුණකි - බොහෝ විට එය පවා නොවේ.
body_lines: බොහෝ බටහිර නායකයින් හැසිරෙන්නේ ලෝකයේ එක් අර්බුදයක් පවතින ආකාරයට ය: රුසියාව යුක්රේනය ආක්‍රමණය කිරීම. ආහාර සහ බලශක්ති සුරක්ෂිතභාවය සඳහා ඇති පුලුල්ව පැතිරී ඇති බලපෑම් ගැන ඇතැමුන් අවදි වන අතර, යටින් පවතින අර්බුදය විසඳීම සඳහා කුඩා කලාප පළලක් ඇති බව පෙනේ: COVID-19 වසංගතය, දේශගුණික බිඳවැටීම් සහ පිරිහීම මගින් මෙහෙයවනු ලබන ගෝලීය ආර්ථික බිඳවැටීමක්. අවම වශයෙන් දශකයක්වත් නිර්මාණය වී ඇති ජාත්‍යන්තර දේශපාලන හා ආර්ථික ක්‍රමය.

එක්ව, මෙම අර්බුද රටවල් ගණනාවක් බරපතල අවදානමකට ලක් කර ඇති අතර එම අවදානම් ඒකාධිපතිවාදය සහ දුර්වල පාලනය සමඟ ඡේදනය වන ෆියුස් එකක් දල්වා ඇත. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව ඊට නිදසුනකි. ඉන්දියාව, චීනය සහ ජපානය වැනි රජයේ ණය හිමියන්ට සහ පුද්ගලික බැඳුම්කර හිමියන්ට රට ඩොලර් බිලියන 50 කට වඩා ණයයි - එය තවදුරටත් පොලී ගෙවීම් නොකරයි.

එහි ආර්ථික දුෂ්කරතා සඳහා හේතු සංකීර්ණ වේ. ශ්‍රී ලංකා ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂගේ පවුල ආර්ථික අවපාලනය, දූෂණය සහ 2009 සිවිල් යුද්ධය අවසන් කිරීමේ කුරිරු ව්‍යාපාරය සම්බන්ධයෙන් විවේචනය කරන ලද ජනතාවාදී, ආරක්ෂාව කේන්ද්‍ර කරගත් පාලන තන්ත්‍රයක් හරහා මෑත ඉතිහාසයේ බොහෝ කාලයක් රට තුළ ආධිපත්‍යය දැරීය. යුධ අපරාධ සහ මානව හිමිකම් උල්ලංඝණය කිරීම් පිලිබඳ විශ්වසනීය චෝදනා පෙන්වා දෙන එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ වාර්තා කිහිපයක විෂයය.

රාජපක්ෂ පවුලේ පාලනය යටතේ, ශ්‍රී ලංකාව කිසිදු ආදායමක් නොලබන සුදු අලි ව්‍යාපෘති සඳහා ඇතුළුව චීන ණය වැලක් දරා ඇත - රාජපක්ෂලාගේ උපන් නගරයට ආසන්න එවැනි එක් ව්‍යාපෘතියක් ෆෝබ්ස් විසින් "ලෝකයේ හිස්ම ජාත්‍යන්තර ගුවන්තොටුපළ" ලෙස නම් කරන ලදී. COVID-19 පහර දුන් විට, සංචාරක ව්‍යාපාරය බිඳවැටීමත් සමඟ ඔවුන් විශාල බදු අඩු කිරීම් සමඟ සීසාන ලදී - රාජ්‍ය ආදායම සහ පුද්ගලික ආදායම් අතුගා දමමින්.

වසර දෙකකින් රටේ විදේශ විනිමය හිඟ වී ඇත. පසුගිය සතියේ, එය එහි අවසන් පැය 24 පෙට්‍රල් තොග දක්වා පහත වැටී ඇත. ඖෂධ සහ ආහාර සැපයුම් ඉතා අඩු මට්ටමක පවතී. ප්‍රමාණවත් අනතුරු ඇඟවීම් සහ නැගී එන විරෝධතා නොතකා, ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදල (IMF) වෙත ළඟා වීමට පෙර ආන්ඩුව බොහෝ වේලාවක් රැඳී සිටි අතර IMF වැඩසටහනක් සාර්ථක වීමේ යථාර්ථවාදී අවස්ථාවක් ලබා දෙන ව්‍යුහාත්මක සහ පාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ කිසිවක් ක්‍රියාත්මක නොවේ. ඒ වෙනුවට පැහැදිලි මහජන වරමක් නොමැති භාරකාර සැකැස්මක් යටතේ ආන්ඩුව දිගටම අරගල කරයි.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දුක්ඛිත තත්ත්වයන් ගෝලීය ප්‍රවණතා මගින් පෝෂණය වන තරමටම එය විසින්ම සාදන ලද ඒවා වන අතර, ඒවා වරකට එක් අර්බුදයක් පමණක් හසුරුවා ගැනීමට හැකි බව පෙනෙන ලෝකයක ඉදිරියට එන දේ පිළිබඳ අශුභ සලකුණකි - බොහෝ විට එය පවා නොවේ.

රුසියා-යුක්රේන යුද්ධයේ සහ COVID-19 හි ප්‍රතිවිපාකයක් ලෙස ඉහල යන ආහාර මිල සහ හිඟය මෙන්ම ආරක්ෂාව විසින් ඇති කරන ලද සැපයුම් දාම කඩාකප්පල් කිරීම්, සංවර්ධනය වෙමින් පවතින ලෝකයේ විශාල කොටසක් ජීවන වියදම් සහ ස්වෛරී නය අර්බුදයක් කරා ගෙන යමින් තිබේ. දරුණු ප්රතිවිපාක.

Bloomberg ට අනුව, "අවම වශයෙන් සංවර්ධනය වෙමින් පවතින ආර්ථිකයන් 14 ක් ... එක්සත් ජනපද භාණ්ඩාගාරවලට වඩා පදනම් ලක්ෂ්‍ය 1,000 ඉක්මවන ණය අස්වැන්නක් ඇත, එය පීඩාවට පත් වූ බැඳුම්කර සඳහා එළිපත්තකි." රටවල් 107 ක බිලියන 1.7 ක ජනතාවක් ආහාර, ඉන්ධන සහ මූල්‍ය අනාරක්ෂිතභාවයේ බරපතල අවදානමක සිටින බවට එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ සංවිධානය අනතුරු අඟවා ඇත: ගෝලීය ජනගහනයෙන් පහෙන් එකක් සහ සංවිධානයේ සාමාජික රටවලින් අඩකට වඩා.

කැළඹිලිවලට මුහුණ දෙන්නේ ඇයිද යන්න පිළිබඳව සෑම රටකටම විවිධ කථා තිබේ. සාමාන්‍යයෙන් යහපාලනයක් සහිත මනා කළමනාකරණයක් සහිත ආර්ථිකයක් ඇති ඝානාව එහි ගුණධර්ම නිසා අමාරුවේ වැටී ඇත. එය වාණිජමය වශයෙන් ණයට ගත හැකි අතර, එය එසේ කළ අතර, එය දැන් ණය අර්බුදයකට මුහුණ දී සිටී. ධනවත් රටවල් ද ප්‍රතිශක්තියක් නොවේ. මාර්තු මාසයේදී අධික බලශක්ති පිරිවැය ප්‍රංශයේ, ග්‍රීසියේ සහ ස්පාඤ්ඤයේ වැඩ වර්ජන හා විරෝධතාවලට තුඩු දුන්නේය.

රුසියාව විසින් යුක්රේනය ආක්‍රමණය කිරීම හේතුවෙන් දේශගුණික පීඩනය නාටකාකාර ලෙස මිල ඉහළ යාමත් සමඟ ඒකාබද්ධ වන බැවින් කලින් තිරිඟු අපනයනකරුවෙකු වූ ඊජිප්තුව දැන් ලෝකයේ විශාලතම ආනයනකරු වේ. අර්බුදයට පෙර රුසියාව සහ යුක්රේනය ගෝලීය තිරිඟු අපනයනයෙන් සියයට 30ක් සැපයූ අතර යුක්රේනය ලෝක ආහාර වැඩසටහනේ සූරියකාන්ත තෙල් සහ අනෙකුත් ආහාර සපයන විශාලතම සැපයුම්කරුවන්ගෙන් එකකි.
මෙම රටවල් බොහොමයක් එකම හදිසි වාට්ටුවේ අවසන් වීමට ඉඩ ඇත. තවද, COVID-19 හමුවේ අපගේ සෞඛ්‍ය සේවා මෙන් ම, අපගේ ගෝලීය මූල්‍ය රෝහල, උපකරණ (අරමුදල්) සහ කාර්ය මණ්ඩලය (නායකයින්) හිඟයකින් පිරී ඇත.

සංවර්ධනය වෙමින් පවතින රටවල පුරවැසියන් ඉල්ලා සිටින දේශපාලන, ආර්ථික සහ පාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණවලට සහාය දෙන අතරම පුලුල්ව පැතිරුණු දුක් වේදනා වැළැක්වීමට එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ සංවිධානය සහ ලෝක බැංකුව වැනි බහුපාර්ශ්වික ආයතන ක්‍රියා කළ යුතුය.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ, සමාන තත්වයන්ට මුහුණ දෙන අනෙකුත් රටවල් මෙන්ම, එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ සංවිධානයට, ලෝක බැංකුවට සහ ආසියානු සංවර්ධන බැංකුවට කෙටි කාලීනව අර්බුදය සමනය කිරීමට පියවර ගත හැකිය. එහෙත්, ආධාර හරවා යැවීමේ ඉතිහාසයක් ඇති ශ්‍රී ලංකාව වැනි රටක, භාණ්ඩ හා සේවා විනිවිදභාවයෙන් හා වගකීමෙන් යුතුව බෙදාහැරීම සහතික කිරීම සඳහා සහ සියලු ප්‍රජාවන් වෙත ළඟාවීම සහතික කිරීම සඳහා ඔවුන් ඔවුන්ගේ සහයෝගයේ කොන්දේසි පෙළගැස්විය යුතුය. එසේ නොවුවහොත්, අවශ්‍යතා ඇති පුද්ගලයින් වෙනුවට දූෂිත දේශපාලකයන්ට ඇප දීමේ අවදානමක් ඇත.

මධ්‍ය කාලීනව, ශ්‍රී ලංකාව සඳහා ශක්‍ය ආර්ථික හා දේශපාලන අනාගතයක් නිර්මාණය කරන ප්‍රතිපත්ති සම්පාදනය කිරීම සඳහා පුළුල් සහයෝගයක් ඇති විශ්වසනීය රජයකට ඉඩ සලසන කඩඉමක් මෙම සහනයට ඇතුළත් විය යුතුය. මෙහිදී IMF ප්‍රධාන කාර්යභාරයක් ඉටු කරයි. එය ශ්‍රී ලාංකික ජනතාව තම හවුල්කරුවන් ලෙස දැකිය යුතු අතර එහි පැකේජයේ කොටසක් ලෙස ඇතුළත් කර ඇති පාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ඔවුන්ගේ කැමැත්ත පිළිබිඹු කරන බවට සහතික විය යුතුය. ලෝක බැංකුව, අනෙක් අතට, මෙම ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ යථාර්ථයක් බවට පත් කිරීම සඳහා තාක්ෂණික සහාය සහ මූල්‍යකරණය සඳහා උපකාර කළ හැකිය.

මේ වන විටත් බොහෝ අපකීර්තියට පත් වූ විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය ඉවත් කිරීම, ආයතනවල ස්වාධීනත්වය වැඩි කිරීම, දූෂණ විරෝධී ආයතන බලගැන්වීම, සොරකම් කළ රජයේ වත්කම් පසුපස යෑම වැනි සංවේදී ක්‍රියාමාර්ග ගත යුතු යැයි සිවිල් සමාජ කණ්ඩායම් ඉල්ලා තිබේ. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ ඝාතනයට ලක් වූ, වධහිංසාවට ලක් වූ සහ අතුරුදන් වූ දස දහස් ගණනකට යුක්තිය ඉල්ලා කෙරෙන ඉල්ලීම් ද ඝෝෂාකාරී වෙමින් පවතී. ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදල අවධානයෙන් සිටිය යුතු අතර ඕනෑම ගනුදෙනුවක-ඕනෑම රටක ජනතාව තම සැබෑ සහකරු ලෙස සැලකිය යුතුය. සියල්ලට පසු, අවසානයේ IMF ආපසු ගෙවන්නේ මහජනතාවයි.

දිගුකාලීනව, ශ්‍රී ලංකාව සහ අනෙකුත් ජාතීන්ට ණය ප්‍රතිව්‍යුහගත කිරීමක් අවශ්‍ය වනු ඇත. මෙහිදී, ණයහිමි රටවල් සහ විශේෂයෙන්ම G-20, ශ්‍රී ලංකාව වැනි මධ්‍යම ආදායම් ලබන රටවලට ඉතා අඩු, ප්‍රමාද වූ සහ කිසිසේත්ම කිසිවක් ලබා දී නොමැති "පොදු රාමුව" මත වැඩිදියුණු කළ යුතුය. අවම වශයෙන්, ප්‍රතිව්‍යුහගතකරණයට පිවිසෙන ඕනෑම රටක් ක්ෂණික ණය ආපසු ගෙවීමේ ඇනහිටීමක් අපේක්ෂා කළ යුතු අතර අවසානයේ ණය අඩු කිරීම් ආර්ථික වර්ධනය සඳහා යථාර්ථවාදී සැලැස්මකට ගැලපේ. පුද්ගලික ණයහිමියන් සහ චීනය සහභාගී විය යුතු අතර, ඔවුන් එසේ නොකරන්නේ නම්, ජාමූඅ කෙසේ හෝ රටට ණය දීමට පියවර ගත යුතුය, එය රඳවා තබා ඇති අය ආපසු නොගෙවන කොන්දේසිය මත.

එවැනි ක්‍රියාවක් ධනවත් රටවල දේශපාලන සහයෝගය මත පුරෝකථනය කර ඇත. නමුත් බොහෝ ආන්ඩු අහක බලාගෙන ඉන්නවා, යුක්රේනය මත දේශප්‍රේමයේ රැල්ලක් පැදවීමට උත්සාහ කරන නමුත් මෙම අනෙක් පෙරමුණේ පරිමාණයෙන් ප්‍රතිචාර දැක්වීමට දැක්ම, කැමැත්ත, කුසලතා හෝ කලාප පළලක් නොමැතිව, එය සමහර විට රුසියාවේ ආක්‍රමණය තරම්ම ගෝලීය ස්ථාවරත්වයට තර්ජනය කරයි. දිනන්න නම් අපි ඒ දෙකෙන්ම ජය ගත යුතුයි.

පසුගිය සතියේ, G-20 මුදල් ඇමතිවරුන්ට නය මත ප්‍රගතිය සඳහා අවස්ථාවක් අහිමි විය. එක්සත් ජනපදය ආහාර අර්බුදය සම්බන්ධයෙන් එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ සංවිධානයේ වැදගත් විවාදයකට නායකත්වය දුන් අතර, සාකච්ඡාව දොස් පැවරීමේ ක්‍රීඩාවකට බැස ගියේය. ප්‍රකාශ කරන ලද ක්‍රියාමාර්ග සාදරයෙන් පිළිගනිමු, නමුත් ණය සහන සහ දේශගුණික මූල්‍ය කටයුතු පසෙක තබා, තවමත් COVID-19 එන්නත් සඳහා බලා සිටින මිනිසුන් විසින් ඒවා නරුමත්වයෙන් පිළිගනු ඇත.

2021 දී, IMF සාමාජිකයින්ට ණය ගැනීමට භාවිතා කළ හැකි ණය රේඛාවක් ලබා දීමෙන් ගෝලීය ද්‍රවශීලතාවය ඉහළ නැංවීම සඳහා IMF විසින් වාර්තාගත ඩොලර් බිලියන 650 ක විශේෂ ඇඳීම් හිමිකම් නිකුත් කරන ලදී. එහෙත් එවැනි අරමුදල් අඩු ආදායම්ලාභී රටවලට ලබා දීමට පොහොසත් රටක පොරොන්දු මින් බෙහෙවින් අඩු වී ඇති අතර සතයක්වත් ලබා දී නොමැත.

පසුගිය වසරේ ප්‍රවෘත්ති චක්‍රයේ ආධිපත්‍යය දැරූ ඇෆ්ගනිස්තානය පවා බොහෝ දුරට අමතක වී ඇති බවක් පෙනේ, එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ මානුෂීය ආයාචනය සමඟින් අඩක් පමණක් අරමුදල් සපයා ඇත, එහි සිටින මිනිසුන්ගෙන් සියයට 95 ක් කුසගින්නේ සිටින අතර, ළමයින් සහ අවයව විකුණන පුද්ගලයින්ගේ කම්පන සහගත වාර්තා සමඟ. මේ අතර, චැඩ් සහ දකුණු සුඩානය වැනි රටවල් ගෝලීය න්‍යාය පත්‍රයට ඇතුළත් වන්නේ කලාතුරකිනි.

අපට එකවර එක දෙයකට වඩා අවධානය යොමු කිරීමට හැකි විය යුතුය. එහෙත්, සියලු අංශවල පාර්ශ්වකරුවන් කැඳවන ගෝලීය හදිසි වේදිකාවක් හෝ මානුෂීය ආධාර මාධ්‍ය ආවරණය මත පුරෝකථනය නොකිරීම සහතික කිරීම සඳහා නම්‍යශීලී අරමුදල් වැනි ප්‍රතිචාර දැක්වීමේ හැකියාව වැඩි කරන යාන්ත්‍රණයන් අපි දිගටම කරගෙන යමු. මානව හිමිකම් සහ ප‍්‍රජාතන්ත‍්‍රවාදී පාලනයට සහාය දීමේ වැඩසටහන් දුක්ඛිත ලෙස අඩුවෙන් අරමුදල් සපයා ඇත.

මෙහි එක්සත් රාජධානියේ, පෙර නිමිති හොඳකට වඩා අඩු ය: යුක්රේනයේ ඵලදායී චියර්ලීඩර් වූ රජයක් චීනයේ ආධාර සහ වෙළඳ ආකෘතියේ දකින දේ, ඉතා කුඩා පරිමාණයෙන් අනුකරණය කරන, අශෝභන නව සංවර්ධන උපාය මාර්ගයක් නිවේදනය කර ඇත. විදේශ ලේකම් Liz Truss පවසන්නේ එක්සත් රාජධානිය "අපහාසාත්මක නළුවන්ගේ" උපක්‍රම පිළිබිඹු නොකරන නමුත් එය "ඔවුන්ට ගැලපෙන" බවයි.

මෙම කියවීම පුණ්‍යක්ෂම නොවන්නේ නම්, ගෝලීය ආහාර අර්බුදයට ඔවුන්ගේ ප්‍රතිචාරය “පරිමාණ” කරන ලෙස ජාත්‍යන්තර ආයතනවලින් ඉල්ලා සිටින අතරම, තමන් කීර්තිමත් වූ දරිද්‍රතා විරෝධී වැඩ අත්හැරීමට සහ එහි බහුපාර්ශ්වික ආධාර වියදම් අඩකට ආසන්න ප්‍රමාණයක් අඩු කිරීමට එක්සත් රාජධානියේ තීරණයද එසේමය. .

මේ වන විටත් එහි ජාත්‍යන්තර සංවර්ධන අමාත්‍යාංශය වසා දමා විදේශ කාර්යාලයට කණගාටුව ඉතිරි කර ඇති රාජ්‍ය සේවය කප්පාදු කිරීම සඳහා මතවාදීමය වශයෙන් මෙහෙයවනු ලබන තවත් අභ්‍යාසයකින් මෙය සංකීර්ණ වී ඇත. වත්මන් දුක්ඛිත තත්වයට තුඩු දී ඇති අසාර්ථක පාලන තන්ත්‍රයන් ශක්තිමත් නොකිරීමට ස්ථාවරත්වය සඳහා වන ගවේෂණය සහතික කළ යුතු එකම රට ශ්‍රී ලංකාව නොවේ.

මෙම අභියෝග අතිමහත් ය: සීමා සහිත සම්පත් ලෝකයක්, රාජ්‍ය ධාරිතාව අඩුවීම සහ හිටපු එක්සත් ජනපද ජනාධිපති ඩොනල්ඩ් ට්‍රම්ප් විසින් බිඩෙන් පරිපාලනයට එල්ල කරන ලද නැවත නැගී එන තර්ජනය වැනි යුරෝපීය සංගමයේ සහ එක්සත් ජනපදයේ වැඩෙන දේශීය ගැටලු. සංඥා අශුභයි: ජාත්‍යන්තර COVID-19 අරමුදල් ප්‍රතිස්ථාපනය කිරීමේ උත්සාහයන් පසුගිය සතියේ එක්සත් ජනපද කොන්ග්‍රසය විසින් නැවත වරක් අවහිර කරන ලදී. වොෂින්ටනය සහ එහි බටහිර සගයන් වර්තමානයේ එක් ගැටලුවක් සහිත නගර වේ. අර්බුද වැඩි වන විට, ඔවුන්ට මෙම දුරදිග නොබලා සිටීමට නොහැකිය.

ta.title: இலங்கையின் துயரங்கள் உலகப் போக்குகளால் தூண்டப்பட்டதைப் போலவே அதன் சொந்த தயாரிப்பாக இருந்தாலும், அவை ஒரு நேரத்தில் ஒரு நெருக்கடியைக் கையாளக்கூடியதாகத் தோன்றும் உலகில் என்ன வரப்போகிறது என்பதற்கான அச்சுறுத்தலாகும் - பெரும்பாலும் அதுவும் இல்லை.
body_lines: பல மேற்கத்திய தலைவர்கள் உலகில் ஒரு நெருக்கடி இருப்பது போல் நடந்து கொள்கிறார்கள்: உக்ரைன் மீதான ரஷ்யாவின் படையெடுப்பு. உணவு மற்றும் எரிசக்தி பாதுகாப்பிற்கான பரவலான நாக்-ஆன் விளைவுகளுக்கு சிலர் விழித்துக்கொண்டாலும், அடிப்படையான நெருக்கடியை எதிர்கொள்ள சிறிய அலைவரிசை இருப்பதாகத் தோன்றுகிறது: கோவிட்-19 தொற்றுநோய், காலநிலை சீர்குலைவு மற்றும் சீரழிவு ஆகியவற்றால் உந்தப்பட்ட உலகளாவிய பொருளாதாரம். குறைந்தபட்சம் ஒரு தசாப்தமாக உருவாக்கப்பட்ட சர்வதேச அரசியல் மற்றும் பொருளாதார அமைப்பு.

ஒன்றாக, இந்த நெருக்கடிகள் பல நாடுகளை கடுமையான ஆபத்தில் ஆழ்த்தியுள்ளன, மேலும் அந்த அபாயங்கள் சர்வாதிகாரம் மற்றும் மோசமான நிர்வாகத்துடன் குறுக்கிடும் இடத்தில் ஒரு உருகியை எரியவிட்டன. இலங்கை ஒரு உதாரணம். இந்தியா, சீனா மற்றும் ஜப்பான் போன்ற அரசாங்கக் கடன் வழங்குநர்கள் மற்றும் தனியார் பத்திரதாரர்களுக்கு நாடு $50 பில்லியனுக்கும் மேல் கடன்பட்டுள்ளது - மேலும் அது இனி வட்டி செலுத்துவதில்லை.

அதன் பொருளாதார நெருக்கடிக்கான காரணங்கள் சிக்கலானவை. இலங்கை ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்சவின் குடும்பம் அதன் சமீப கால வரலாற்றில் பொருளாதார முறைகேடு, ஊழல் மற்றும் 2009 இல் உள்நாட்டுப் போரை முடிவுக்குக் கொண்டுவருவதற்கான அதன் கொடூரமான பிரச்சாரத்திற்காக விமர்சிக்கப்பட்ட ஜனரஞ்சக, பாதுகாப்பை மையமாகக் கொண்ட ஆட்சியின் மூலம் நாட்டில் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்துகிறது. பல ஐக்கிய நாடுகளின் அறிக்கைகளின் பொருள், இது போர்க்குற்றங்கள் மற்றும் மனித உரிமை மீறல்கள் பற்றிய நம்பகமான குற்றச்சாட்டுகளை சுட்டிக்காட்டுகிறது.

ராஜபக்ச குடும்பத்தின் ஆட்சியின் கீழ், சிறிலங்கா வெள்ளை யானைத் திட்டங்களுக்காக சீனக் கடன்களைச் சுமத்தியுள்ளது, அது வருமானம் ஈட்டவில்லை - ராஜபக்சவின் சொந்த ஊருக்கு அருகில் உள்ள அத்தகைய திட்டமானது ஃபோர்ப்ஸால் "உலகின் காலியான சர்வதேச விமான நிலையம்" என்று அழைக்கப்பட்டது. கோவிட்-19 தாக்கியபோது, ​​சுற்றுலாத்துறை வீழ்ச்சியடைந்ததால் அவர்கள் பெரும் வரிக் குறைப்புகளுடன் உழன்றனர் - மாநில வருவாய் மற்றும் தனிநபர் வருமானத்தை அழித்துவிட்டனர்.

இரண்டு வருடங்களில் நாட்டில் அந்நியச் செலாவணி இல்லாமல் போய்விட்டது. கடந்த வாரம், அதன் கடைசி 24 மணிநேர பெட்ரோல் பங்குகள் குறைந்துள்ளது. மருந்து மற்றும் உணவு விநியோகம் மிகவும் குறைவாக உள்ளது. ஏராளமான எச்சரிக்கைகள் மற்றும் பெருகிவரும் எதிர்ப்புகள் இருந்தபோதிலும், சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்தை (IMF) அணுகுவதற்கு முன்பு அரசாங்கம் நீண்ட காலம் காத்துக்கொண்டது, மேலும் IMF திட்டம் வெற்றிபெறுவதற்கான யதார்த்தமான வாய்ப்பை அனுமதிக்கும் கட்டமைப்பு மற்றும் நிர்வாக சீர்திருத்தங்கள் எதுவும் நடைமுறையில் இல்லை. மாறாக, தெளிவான பொது ஆணை இல்லாத ஒரு பராமரிப்பாளர் அமைப்பின் கீழ் அரசாங்கம் தொடர்ந்து போராடுகிறது.

இலங்கையின் துயரங்கள் உலகப் போக்குகளால் தூண்டப்பட்டதைப் போலவே அதன் சொந்த தயாரிப்பாக இருந்தாலும், அவை ஒரு நேரத்தில் ஒரு நெருக்கடியைக் கையாளக்கூடியதாகத் தோன்றும் உலகில் என்ன வரப்போகிறது என்பதற்கான அச்சுறுத்தலாகும் - பெரும்பாலும் அதுவும் இல்லை.

ரஷ்யா-உக்ரைன் போர் மற்றும் கோவிட்-19 ஆகியவற்றின் விளைவாக அதிகரித்து வரும் உணவுப் பொருட்களின் விலைகள் மற்றும் பற்றாக்குறை, அத்துடன் பாதுகாப்பு தூண்டப்பட்ட விநியோகச் சங்கிலி சீர்குலைவுகள், வளரும் நாடுகளின் பெரும்பகுதியை வாழ்க்கைச் செலவு மற்றும் இறையாண்மைக் கடன் நெருக்கடியை நோக்கித் தள்ளுகின்றன. கடுமையான விளைவுகள்.

ப்ளூம்பெர்க்கின் கூற்றுப்படி, "குறைந்தபட்சம் 14 வளரும் பொருளாதாரங்கள் … US Treasuries ஐ விட 1,000 அடிப்படை புள்ளிகளுக்கு மேல் கடன் விளைச்சலைக் கொண்டுள்ளன, இது பத்திரங்கள் துன்பகரமானதாகக் கருதப்படும்." 107 நாடுகளில் உள்ள 1.7 பில்லியன் மக்கள் உணவு, எரிபொருள் மற்றும் நிதிப் பாதுகாப்பின்மையால் கடுமையான ஆபத்தில் உள்ளனர்: உலக மக்கள்தொகையில் ஐந்தில் ஒரு பங்கு மற்றும் அமைப்பின் உறுப்பு நாடுகளில் பாதிக்கும் மேற்பட்டவர்கள் என்று ஐ.நா. எச்சரித்துள்ளது.

ஒவ்வொரு நாடும் ஏன் கொந்தளிப்பை எதிர்கொள்கிறது என்பதற்கு வெவ்வேறு கதை உள்ளது. கானா, பொதுவாக நல்ல நிர்வாகத்துடன் நன்கு நிர்வகிக்கப்பட்ட பொருளாதாரம், அதன் நற்பண்புகளால் சிக்கலில் உள்ளது. இது வணிக ரீதியாக கடன் வாங்கலாம், அதனால் அது செய்தது, அது இப்போது கடன் நெருக்கடியை எதிர்கொள்கிறது. பணக்கார நாடுகளும் நோய் எதிர்ப்பு சக்தி கொண்டவை அல்ல. மார்ச் மாதத்தில், அதிக ஆற்றல் செலவுகள் பிரான்ஸ், கிரீஸ் மற்றும் ஸ்பெயினில் வேலைநிறுத்தங்கள் மற்றும் எதிர்ப்புகளுக்கு வழிவகுத்தன.

முன்னாள் கோதுமை ஏற்றுமதியாளராக இருந்த எகிப்து, உக்ரைன் மீதான ரஷ்யாவின் படையெடுப்பின் காரணமாக காலநிலை அழுத்தங்கள் வியத்தகு விலை அதிகரிப்புடன் இணைந்ததால், இப்போது உலகின் மிகப்பெரிய இறக்குமதியாளராக உள்ளது. நெருக்கடிக்கு முன், ரஷ்யாவும் உக்ரைனும் உலகளாவிய கோதுமை ஏற்றுமதியில் 30 சதவீதத்தை வழங்கின, மேலும் உக்ரைனும் உலக உணவுத் திட்டத்தின் சூரியகாந்தி எண்ணெய் மற்றும் பிற உணவுகளை வழங்கும் மிகப்பெரிய நிறுவனங்களில் ஒன்றாகும்.

இந்த நாடுகளில் பலவும் ஒரே அவசர சிகிச்சை பிரிவில் முடிவடையும் வாய்ப்பு உள்ளது. மேலும், கோவிட்-19-ஐ எதிர்கொள்ளும் நமது சுகாதாரச் சேவைகளைப் போலவே, எங்களின் உலகளாவிய நிதி மருத்துவமனையும், உபகரணங்கள் (நிதிகள்) மற்றும் பணியாளர்கள் (தலைவர்கள்) பற்றாக்குறையாக உள்ளது.

வளரும் நாடுகளில் உள்ள குடிமக்கள் கோரும் அரசியல், பொருளாதார மற்றும் நிர்வாக சீர்திருத்தங்களை ஆதரிக்கும் அதே வேளையில் பரவலான துன்பங்களைத் தடுக்க U.N மற்றும் உலக வங்கி போன்ற பலதரப்பு அமைப்புகள் செயல்பட வேண்டும்.

இதேபோன்ற சூழ்நிலைகளில் தங்களைக் காணும் மற்ற நாடுகளைப் போலவே இலங்கையிலும், ஐ.நா., உலக வங்கி மற்றும் ஆசிய அபிவிருத்தி வங்கி ஆகிய அனைத்தும் குறுகிய காலத்தில் நெருக்கடியைத் தணிக்க நடவடிக்கை எடுக்க முடியும். ஆனால், இலங்கை போன்ற ஒரு நாட்டில் உதவியை திசை திருப்பும் வரலாற்றைக் கொண்ட ஒரு நாட்டில், பொருட்கள் மற்றும் சேவைகள் வெளிப்படையான மற்றும் பொறுப்பான முறையில் வழங்கப்படுவதை உறுதி செய்வதற்கும் அனைத்து சமூகங்களையும் சென்றடைவதை உறுதி செய்வதற்கும் அவர்கள் தங்கள் ஆதரவின் விதிமுறைகளை சீரமைக்க வேண்டும். இல்லையெனில், அவர்கள் தேவைப்படுபவர்களுக்குப் பதிலாக ஊழல் அரசியல்வாதிகளை பிணையில் எடுப்பார்கள்.

நடுத்தர காலத்தில், இந்த நிவாரணமானது இலங்கைக்கு சாத்தியமான பொருளாதார மற்றும் அரசியல் எதிர்காலத்தை உருவாக்கும் கொள்கைகளை இயற்றுவதற்கு பரந்த ஆதரவுடன் நம்பகமான அரசாங்கத்தை அனுமதிக்கும் ஒரு தடையை உள்ளடக்கியதாக இருக்க வேண்டும். இங்கே, IMF முக்கிய பங்கு வகிக்கிறது. அது இலங்கை மக்களை தனது பங்காளிகளாகப் பார்க்க வேண்டும் மற்றும் அதன் தொகுப்பின் ஒரு பகுதியாக உள்ளடக்கப்பட்டுள்ள நிர்வாக சீர்திருத்தங்கள் அவர்களின் விருப்பத்தை பிரதிபலிக்கின்றன என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்த வேண்டும். உலக வங்கி, இந்த சீர்திருத்தங்களை யதார்த்தமாக்குவதற்கு தொழில்நுட்ப ஆதரவு மற்றும் நிதியுதவியுடன் உதவ முடியும்.

ஏற்கனவே, சிவில் சமூகக் குழுக்கள் மிகவும் அவதூறான நிறைவேற்று ஜனாதிபதி முறையை நீக்குதல், நிறுவனங்களின் சுதந்திரத்தை அதிகரிப்பது, ஊழல் எதிர்ப்பு அமைப்புகளுக்கு அதிகாரமளித்தல் மற்றும் திருடப்பட்ட அரசாங்க சொத்துகளைப் பின்தொடர்வது போன்ற விவேகமான நடவடிக்கைகளுக்கு அழைப்பு விடுத்துள்ளன. இலங்கையில் கொல்லப்பட்ட, சித்திரவதை செய்யப்பட்ட, காணாமல் போன பல்லாயிரக்கணக்கான மக்களுக்கு நீதி கிடைக்க வேண்டும் என்ற கோரிக்கைகளும் வலுத்து வருகின்றன. IMF கவனத்துடன் இருக்க வேண்டும் மற்றும் எந்த நாட்டிலும் மக்களை தனது உண்மையான பங்காளியாக கருத வேண்டும். எல்லாவற்றிற்கும் மேலாக, IMF-க்கு இறுதியில் திருப்பிச் செலுத்துவது பொதுமக்கள்தான்.

நீண்ட காலத்திற்கு, இலங்கை மற்றும் பிற நாடுகளுக்கு கடன் மறுசீரமைப்பு தேவைப்படும். இங்கு, கடன் வழங்கும் நாடுகளும், குறிப்பாக G-20 நாடுகளும், தற்போதுள்ள "பொது கட்டமைப்பை" மேம்படுத்த வேண்டும், இது இலங்கை போன்ற நடுத்தர வருமான நாடுகளுக்கு மிகக் குறைவாகவும், தாமதமாகவும், எதையும் வழங்கவில்லை. குறைந்தபட்சம், மறுசீரமைப்பில் நுழையும் எந்தவொரு நாடும் உடனடி கடனைத் திருப்பிச் செலுத்துவதை நிறுத்த வேண்டும் மற்றும் இறுதியில் பொருளாதார வளர்ச்சிக்கான ஒரு யதார்த்தமான திட்டத்துடன் கடன் குறைப்புகளை எதிர்பார்க்க வேண்டும். தனியார் கடனாளிகளும் சீனாவும் பங்கேற்க வேண்டும், அவர்கள் அவ்வாறு செய்யாவிட்டால், IMF நாட்டிற்கு எப்படியும் கடன் கொடுக்க முன்வர வேண்டும், அந்த நிபந்தனையின் பேரில் கடனை வைத்திருப்பவர்கள் திருப்பிச் செலுத்தப்பட மாட்டார்கள்.

இத்தகைய நடவடிக்கை பணக்கார நாடுகளின் அரசியல் ஆதரவை முன்னிறுத்துகிறது. ஆனால் பல அரசாங்கங்கள் உக்ரைன் மீது தேசபக்தியின் அலையை சவாரி செய்ய முயல்கின்றன, ஆனால் பார்வை, விருப்பம், திறமை அல்லது அலைவரிசை இல்லாமல் இந்த மற்ற முனையில் பதிலளிக்கின்றன, இது ரஷ்யாவின் படையெடுப்பைப் போலவே உலகளாவிய ஸ்திரத்தன்மையையும் அச்சுறுத்துகிறது. வெற்றி பெற, இரண்டிலும் வெற்றி பெற வேண்டும்.

கடந்த வாரம், G-20 நிதி அமைச்சர்கள் கடனில் முன்னேற்றத்திற்கான வாய்ப்பை இழந்தனர். உணவு நெருக்கடி குறித்து ஐக்கிய நாடுகள் சபையில் அமெரிக்கா ஒரு முக்கியமான விவாதத்தை நடத்தியபோது, ​​​​அந்த விவாதம் ஒரு பழி விளையாட்டாக இறங்கியது. கூறப்பட்ட நடவடிக்கைகள் வரவேற்கத்தக்கவை, ஆனால் இன்னும் கோவிட்-19 தடுப்பூசிகளுக்காகக் காத்திருக்கும் மக்களால் அவை நிச்சயமாக சிடுமூஞ்சித்தனத்துடன் வரவேற்கப்படும், கடன் நிவாரணம் மற்றும் காலநிலை நிதி ஆகியவை ஒருபுறம் இருக்கட்டும்.

2021 ஆம் ஆண்டில், IMF உறுப்பினர்கள் IMF உறுப்பினர்களுக்கு கடன் வாங்குவதற்குப் பயன்படுத்தக்கூடிய கடன் வரியை வழங்குவதன் மூலம் உலகளாவிய பணப்புழக்கத்தை அதிகரிக்க 650 பில்லியன் டாலர் சிறப்பு வரைதல் உரிமைகளை வெளியிட்டது. ஆனால் குறைந்த வருமானம் கொண்ட நாடுகளுக்கு இத்தகைய நிதி கிடைக்கச் செய்யும் பணக்கார நாடு உறுதிமொழிகள் இதைவிட மிகக் குறைவாகவே உள்ளன, ஒரு சதமும் கூட வழங்கப்படவில்லை.

கடந்த ஆண்டு செய்திச் சுழற்சியில் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்திய ஆப்கானிஸ்தான் கூட மறந்துவிட்டதாகத் தோன்றுகிறது, U.N. இன் மனிதாபிமான முறையீட்டின் மூலம், அங்குள்ள 95 சதவீத மக்கள் பட்டினி கிடப்பதால், குழந்தைகள் மற்றும் உறுப்புகளை விற்கும் நபர்களின் அதிர்ச்சிகரமான அறிக்கைகள் பாதி மட்டுமே நிதியளிக்கப்பட்டன. இதற்கிடையில், சாட் மற்றும் தெற்கு சூடான் போன்ற நாடுகள் அரிதாகவே உலகளாவிய நிகழ்ச்சி நிரலில் இடம் பெறுகின்றன.

நாம் ஒரே நேரத்தில் ஒன்றுக்கு மேற்பட்ட விஷயங்களில் கவனம் செலுத்த வேண்டும். ஆயினும்கூட, பதிலளிப்பதற்கான எங்கள் திறனை அதிகரிக்கும் வழிமுறைகளை நாங்கள் தொடர்ந்து முன்னெடுத்து வருகிறோம், அதாவது உலகளாவிய அவசரகால தளம் போன்ற அனைத்துத் துறைகளிலிருந்தும் பங்குதாரர்களைக் கூட்டுவது அல்லது மனிதாபிமான உதவியை ஊடக கவரேஜில் முன்னறிவிப்பதில்லை என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்த நெகிழ்வான நிதி. மனித உரிமைகள் மற்றும் ஜனநாயக நிர்வாகத்தை ஆதரிப்பதற்கான திட்டங்கள் பரிதாபகரமாக குறைவாகவே உள்ளன.

இங்கே யுனைடெட் கிங்டமில், சகுனங்கள் நல்லதை விட குறைவாகவே உள்ளன: உக்ரைனில் திறம்பட சியர்லீடராக இருந்த அரசாங்கம், சீனாவின் உதவி மற்றும் வர்த்தக மாதிரியில் பார்ப்பதை மிகச் சிறிய அளவில் பிரதிபலிக்கும் ஒரு மோசமான புதிய மேம்பாட்டு உத்தியை அறிவித்துள்ளது. வெளியுறவுச் செயலர் லிஸ் ட்ரஸ் கூறுகையில், "கெட்ட நடிகர்களின்" தந்திரோபாயங்களை இங்கிலாந்து பிரதிபலிக்காது, ஆனால் அது "அவர்களுடன் பொருந்தும்" என்று கூறுகிறார்.

இந்த வாசிப்பு தொண்டு செய்ய முடியாதது என்றால், U.K இன் முடிவும் அது புகழ் பெற்ற வறுமை எதிர்ப்புப் பணியை கைவிடுவது மற்றும் அதன் பலதரப்பு உதவி செலவினங்களை கிட்டத்தட்ட பாதியாகக் குறைக்கும் - அதே நேரத்தில் உலகளாவிய உணவு நெருக்கடிக்கு தங்கள் பதிலை "அளவிட" சர்வதேச நிறுவனங்களை அழைக்கிறது. .

சிவில் சேவையை வெட்டுவதற்கான மற்றொரு கருத்தியல் உந்துதல் பயிற்சியால் இது கூட்டப்பட்டுள்ளது, ஏற்கனவே அதன் சர்வதேச மேம்பாட்டு அமைச்சகத்தை மூடிவிட்டு, மன்னிப்பு அலுவலகத்திற்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. தற்போதைய அவல நிலைக்கு வழிவகுத்துள்ள தோல்வியடைந்த ஆட்சிகளை நிலைநிறுத்துவதற்கான தனது வேட்கையை உறுதிப்படுத்த வேண்டிய ஒரே நாடு இலங்கையல்ல.

இந்த சவால்கள் மகத்தானவை: கட்டுப்படுத்தப்பட்ட வளங்களின் உலகம், குறைக்கப்பட்ட அரசாங்க திறன் மற்றும் ஐரோப்பிய ஒன்றியம் மற்றும் அமெரிக்காவில் பெருகிவரும் உள்நாட்டுப் பிரச்சினைகள், முன்னாள் அமெரிக்க ஜனாதிபதி டொனால்ட் டிரம்ப் பிடன் நிர்வாகத்திற்கு மீண்டும் எழுப்பும் அச்சுறுத்தல் போன்றவை. அறிகுறிகள் சாதகமற்றவை: சர்வதேச COVID-19 நிதியுதவியை மீட்டெடுப்பதற்கான முயற்சிகள் கடந்த வாரம் அமெரிக்க காங்கிரஸால் மீண்டும் தடுக்கப்பட்டன. வாஷிங்டனும் அதன் மேற்கத்திய சகாக்களும் தற்போது ஒரு பிரச்சினை நகரங்களாக உள்ளன. நெருக்கடிகள் பெருகும் போது, ​​அவர்களால் இந்த குறுகிய நோக்கத்துடன் இருக்க முடியாது.

en.title: While Sri Lanka’s woes are as much of its own making as fueled by global trends, they are an ominous marker of what’s to come in a world that seems able to handle just one crisis at a time—and often not even that.
body_lines: Many Western leaders are behaving as though there is one crisis in the world: Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. While some are waking up to the widespread knock-on effects for food and energy security, there is little bandwidth, it seems, to address the underlying looming crisis: a global economic unwinding driven by the COVID-19 pandemic, climate breakdown, and degradation of the international political and economic system that has been at least a decade in the making.

Together, these crises have put scores of countries at serious risk and lit a fuse where those risks intersect with authoritarianism and poor governance. Sri Lanka is a case in point. The country owes over $50 billion to government creditors such as India, China, and Japan, and private bondholders—and it is no longer making interest payments.

The reasons for its economic woes are complex. Sri Lankan President Gotabaya Rajapaksa’s family has dominated the country for much of its recent history through a populist, security-centered regime criticized for economic mismanagement, corruption, and its brutal campaign to end the civil war in 2009. Its conduct during the conflict has been the subject of several United Nations reports, which point to credible allegations of war crimes and human rights abuses.

Under the Rajapaksa family’s rule, Sri Lanka has incurred a string of Chinese debts, including for white elephant projects that have yielded little to no income—one such project close to the Rajapaksas’ hometown was dubbed “The World’s Emptiest International Airport” by Forbes. When COVID-19 struck, they ploughed on with sweeping tax cuts as tourism collapsed—wiping out state revenue and personal incomes.

Two years on, the country has run out of foreign exchange. Last week, it was down to its last 24 hours of gasoline stocks. Medicine and food supplies are critically low. Despite ample warning and mounting protests, the government held out for too long before approaching the International Monetary Fund (IMF), and none of the structural and governance reforms are in place that might allow an IMF program a realistic chance of success. Instead, the government continues to struggle under a caretaker setup that lacks a clear public mandate.

While Sri Lanka’s woes are as much of its own making as fueled by global trends, they are an ominous marker of what’s to come in a world that seems able to handle just one crisis at a time—and often not even that.

Rising food prices and shortages, a consequence of the Russia-Ukraine war and COVID-19, as well as security-induced supply chain disruptions, are driving large parts of the developing world toward a cost-of-living and sovereign debt crisis with likely drastic consequences.

According to Bloomberg, “At least 14 developing economies … have debt yields at an excess of 1,000 basis points over US Treasuries, a threshold for bonds to be considered distressed.” The U.N. has warned that 1.7 billion people in 107 countries are at serious risk of food, fuel, and financial insecurity: a fifth of the global population and more than half of the organization’s member states.

Each country has a different story for why it faces turmoil. Ghana, a well-managed economy with generally good governance, is in trouble because of its virtues. It could borrow commercially, so it did, and it now faces a debt crisis. Rich countries are also not immune. In March, high energy costs led to strikes and protests in France, Greece, and Spain.

Egypt, a former wheat exporter, is now the world’s biggest importer as climate pressures combine with dramatic price increases due to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Before the crisis, Russia and Ukraine supplied 30 percent of global wheat exports, and Ukraine is also one of the World Food Program’s biggest suppliers of sunflower oil and other foods.

Many of these countries are likely to end up in the same emergency ward. And, a bit like our health services in the face of COVID-19, our global financial hospital is overwhelmed, with equipment (funds) and staff (leaders) in short supply.

Multilateral bodies such as the U.N. and World Bank must act to prevent widespread suffering while supporting political, economic, and governance reforms that citizens across developing countries are demanding.

In Sri Lanka, as with other countries that will find themselves in similar circumstances, the U.N., the World Bank, and the Asian Development Bank can all take action to mitigate the crisis in the short term. But, in a country like Sri Lanka with a history of diverting aid, they must align the terms of their support to ensure goods and services are delivered in a transparent and accountable manner—and reach all communities. Otherwise, they risk bailing out corrupt politicians instead of people in need.

In the medium term, this relief must include an offramp that allows for a credible government with broad support to enact policies that will create a viable economic and political future for Sri Lanka. Here, the IMF plays a key role. It should see the Sri Lankan people as its partners and ensure that the governance reforms included as part of its package reflect their will. The World Bank, in turn, can help with technical support and financing to help these reforms become reality.

Already, civil society groups have called for sensible measures such as eliminating the much-maligned executive presidency, increasing the independence of institutions, empowering anti-corruption bodies, and going after stolen government assets. Calls for justice for the tens of thousands of people killed, tortured, and disappeared in Sri Lanka are also growing louder. The IMF should take heed and consider the people its true partner in any deal—in any country.  After all, it is the public that will ultimately repay the IMF.

In the longer term, Sri Lanka and other nations will require debt restructuring. Here, creditor nations, and the G-20 in particular, must improve upon the existing “Common Framework,” which has offered too little, too late, and nothing at all to middle-income countries such as Sri Lanka. At a minimum, any country that enters restructuring should expect an immediate debt repayment standstill and ultimately debt reductions matched to a realistic plan for economic growth. Private creditors and China must participate, and if they do not, the IMF should step in to lend to the country anyway, on the condition that those holding out are not repaid.

Such action is predicated on political support by rich countries. But too many governments are looking away, trying to ride a wave of patriotism over Ukraine but without the vision, will, skill, or bandwidth to respond at scale on this other front, which threatens global stability perhaps as much as Russia’s invasion. To win, we need to prevail on both.

Last week, G-20 finance ministers missed an opportunity for progress on debt. While the United States led an important debate at the U.N. on the food crisis, the discussion descended into a blame game. The measures touted are welcome, but they will surely be greeted with cynicism by people still waiting for COVID-19 vaccines, let alone debt relief and climate finance.

In 2021, the IMF issued a record $650 billion of Special Drawing Rights to boost global liquidity by giving IMF members a line of credit they can use to borrow against. But rich-country pledges to make such funds available to low-income countries have fallen far short of this, and not a cent has been disbursed.

Even Afghanistan, which dominated the news cycle last year, appears largely forgotten, with the U.N.’s humanitarian appeal only half funded as 95 percent of people there go hungry, with shocking reports of people selling children and organs. Meanwhile, countries like Chad and South Sudan rarely make it onto the global agenda.

We must be able to focus on more than one thing at the same time. And yet we continue to prevaricate on mechanisms that would increase our capacity for response, such as a global emergency platform that would convene stakeholders from all sectors, or flexible funding to ensure humanitarian aid is not predicated on media coverage. Programs to support human rights and democratic governance are woefully underfunded.

Here in the United Kingdom, the omens are less than good: A government that has been an effective cheerleader on Ukraine has announced a dismal new development strategy that mimics, on a much smaller scale, what it sees in China’s aid and trade model. Foreign Secretary Liz Truss says the U.K. won’t mirror the tactics of “malign actors” but that it will “match them.”

If this reading is uncharitable, so is the U.K.’s decision to desert the anti-poverty work for which it was renowned and nearly halve its multilateral aid spending—while calling on international institutions to “scale up” their response to the global food crisis.

This is compounded by yet another ideologically driven exercise to slash the civil service, having already shuttered its international development ministry and folded the sorry remains into the Foreign Office. Sri Lanka is not the only country that must ensure its quest for stability does not entrench the failing regimes that have led to the current plight.

These challenges are immense: a world of constrained resources, diminished governmental capacity, and mounting domestic issues in the European Union and United States, such as the resurgent threat posed by former U.S. President Donald Trump to the Biden administration. The signs are inauspicious: Efforts to restore international COVID-19 funding were stymied again by the U.S. Congress last week. Washington and its Western counterparts are one-issue towns at present. As crises multiply, they cannot afford to be this short-sighted.
